model_explanations.Linear Regression: Predicts a linear relationship between features and performance. Simple, fast, and interpretable but may miss complex patterns.
model_explanations.Random Forest: Uses multiple decision trees to make predictions. Handles complex patterns well and is robust to outliers. Generally more accurate than Linear Regression.
model_explanations.Support Vector Machine: Finds the best boundary between classes. Works well with small datasets and high-dimensional data. Requires feature scaling.

school.why_chosen: Random Forest achieved the highest R² score of 0.9999 on school student data, meaning it best explains the variance in school performance patterns.

model_explanations.Random Forest: Uses multiple decision trees to make predictions. Handles complex patterns well and is robust to outliers. Generally more accurate than Linear Regression.

college.why_chosen: Random Forest achieved the highest R² score of 0.9998 on college student data, meaning it best explains the variance in college performance patterns.
